summary: Full end-to-end validation of Atlas — MT5 Quant Supervisor in MOCK MODE. 13/13 backend pytest tests passed and all UI interactions (account selection, kill switch, risk limits save, alert ACK, ACK ALL, refresh feed/sim tick, equity/drawdown charts) work with no console errors and no failed XHRs.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~455 lines and mixes mock data generation, models, routes, MT5 routing, health, frontend static serving and CORS middleware. CORS middleware is added AFTER routes — fine for FastAPI but conventionally added before include_router; verify no regressions when more middleware is added.

CORS allow_origins defaults to '*' with allow_credentials=True — browsers will reject credentialed CORS with wildcard origins. Not a problem today (no auth, no cookies), but should be tightened before production.

Mock state (ACCOUNTS, ALERTS, kill_switch toggle, risk_limits updates) is purely in-memory and reset on backend restart. This is documented as expected mock behaviour but the test report flags it for awareness.
GET /api/accounts returns a bare JSON list — fine for the dashboard, but inconsistent with /api/alerts which returns {count, alerts}. Consider standardising on an envelope.
Decorative nav tabs (Strategies/Risk/Reports/Audit) have data-testids but no onClick handler. Confirmed by request — expected MVP limitation, not a defect.
System panel values (MT5 Bridge CONNECTED, Telegram ENABLED, API Latency 42ms, Strategies Loaded 6) are static/hardcoded in mock mode — expected, not a bug.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: None. Backend boots with 8 deterministic mock accounts (ACC-001..ACC-008) and 10 mock alerts (ALT-0000..ALT-0009).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is in mock mode (MT5_BRIDGE_URL unset, ATLAS_STORE=mongo). Mock state is in-memory and resets on supervisor restart, so kill-switch/risk-limits/alert-ack mutations from previous runs do not carry over. Account IDs are ACC-001..ACC-008 and alert IDs are ALT-0000..ALT-0009. Pytest suite is at /app/backend/tests/test_atlas_api.py — run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_atlas_api.py -v.
verified_endpoints: GET /api/accounts/{id}/trades?limit=100 -> trades list capped at limit
POST /api/alerts/{id}/ack -> persists acknowledged=true; 404 for unknown id
POST /api/accounts/{id}/kill-switch -> toggles kill_switch + status (LIVE<->PAUSED), persisted on GET
PUT /api/accounts/{id}/risk-limits -> updates and persists all 3 fields
verified_ui_flows: Dashboard loads at REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL root with all panels (kpi-ticker, accounts-table, risk-panel, alerts-panel, system-panel, equity-panel, drawdown-panel, trades-panel)
8 account rows rendered; clicking account-row-ACC-002 updates Risk/Equity/Drawdown/Trades panels to ACC-002
kill-switch-button toggles label 'KILL SWITCH' <-> 'RESUME TRADING' with backend 200
risk-max-daily-loss / risk-max-position-size / risk-max-open-positions inputs editable; risk-save-button triggers PUT /risk-limits => 200
alert-ack-{id} click => POST /alerts/{id}/ack => 200; alert-ack-all clears all single ACK buttons
Equity and Drawdown panels render Recharts SVG with data
Decorative nav tabs (nav-strategies/nav-risk/nav-reports/nav-audit) do not crash and keep Overview visible — expected MVP behaviour
No JS console errors, no failed XHR (>=400) during full session
rca of the issue: No issues found. Both backend and frontend pass end-to-end validation in mock mode.
